_comment: EDITABLE RULE TEMPLATES. A finance user changes the values, not the code. These read in human language; they are NOT parsed from human language (that engine was killed on purpose). Each rule is deterministic — same input always gives same output — because real money cannot run on probability.

R-001.name: Duplicate payment check
R-001.plain_language: Do not pay the same invoice twice. Compare every incoming line-item against (1) the paid-payments ledger and (2) other line-items in the same batch.
R-001._note_on_no_anchor: If a line-item cannot be compared to anything (unknown vendor, no history), do NOT guess clean or duplicate — flag 'unknown' and send to a human.

R-002.name: Bank-account-change fraud signal
R-002.plain_language: If the bank account on an incoming payment does not match the vendor's account on file in master data, flag it. A changed payee account is the classic invoice-redirection fraud.

R-003.name: Threshold escalation routing
R-003.plain_language: Route each request to the approval level its amount requires. Bigger amounts need higher sign-off. This proves the routing engine works without rebuilding the full 5-level paper chain.
